title: UDLM Composite Service Catalog Item
description: The catalog-level Composite Service definition (entities/composite-service-model.md): constituent resource types, their dependency edges, their declared data-movement bindings, and their failure classification — everything DCM needs to expand a catalog request into a Composite Entity. This is item (2) of the [D8] machine-validatable-surface program (data-model-core §2) and the schema evidence for the application model: an 'application' IS a Composite catalog item. Cross-file constraints (depends_on/bindings.from_component must reference sibling component_ids; the depends_on graph must be acyclic; a binding's from_component must appear in that constituent's depends_on) are not expressible in pure JSON Schema — registry/tools/validate.py enforces them as semantic checks.

record_type.description: Top-level dispatch discriminator. Instance-record kinds are distinguished by record_type going forward; resource_type (realized entities) and group_class (DCMGroups) remain the legacy discriminators for the pre-existing record kinds. registry/tools/validate.py dispatches on record_type first.

uuid.description: Stable RFC 9562 v4 identity of this catalog item, minted once at creation (identifier-scheme §2.1; version nibble + variant bits pattern-enforced). Composite Entities produced from this item reference it — never the reverse.

handle.description: ADVISORY human-readable identity (contracts/identifier-scheme.md §3), e.g. org/catalog/three-tier-web-app. The uuid is the authoritative identity; never resolve by handle alone.

name.description: Human-readable name of the Composite Service, e.g. ApplicationStack.WebApp.

version.description: Major.Minor.Revision of this catalog item (registry/VERSIONING.md semantics). Consumers pin catalog_ref to a version.

status.description: Catalog lifecycle status: active | deprecated | retired, with the universal deprecation fields when deprecated (same shape as dcm-group.schema.json).

deprecation_date.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

sunset_date.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

replacement_uuid.description: The successor catalog item, if deprecated.

tenant_uuid.description: REQUIRED owning Tenant — the uuid of a tenant_boundary DCMGroup (registry/dcm-group.schema.json), consistent with [D3] (data-model-core §5): every record belongs to exactly one Tenant. Ownership only — consumption of this catalog item by other tenants happens via requests and cross-tenant authorization grants, never by shared ownership.

composition_visibility.description: How constituents surface as DCM entities (composite-service-model.md §5): opaque = composite uuid only; transparent = every constituent is a first-class entity with a deterministic uuid (CMP-007); selective = only selective_visible constituents surface.

selective_visible.description: Only when composition_visibility = selective: the component_ids surfaced as DCM entities (composite-service-model.md §5). Must reference declared component_ids (registration-time check; not enforced by validate.py yet).

constituents.description: The composite payload declaration (composite-service-model.md §2.1): which constituent resource types make up the service, how they order (depends_on), how data moves between them (bindings), who fulfills each one (provided_by), and how each one's failure affects the composite (failure_effect). component_ids must be unique within the item; depends_on and bindings.from_component must reference sibling component_ids; the depends_on graph must be acyclic (CMP-002/§2.3) — registry/tools/validate.py enforces all three.

component_id.description: Stable local identifier for the constituent within this composite — unique within the item (validate.py-enforced). Used in depends_on references, runtime constituent_status, and transparent-mode uuid derivation (CMP-007).

resource_type.description: Tier-1 vendor-neutral Category.Type name from the Resource Type Registry; single-segment permitted for cross-cutting types — exactly the same name grammar as the realized-entity schema (data-model-core §2).

type_version.description: Optional pin to a specific Resource Type Specification version. Omitted = the registry's current version at expansion time.

provided_by.description: self = the registering provider fulfills this constituent directly via its standard Services API; external = DCM's Placement Engine selects a provider at request time (composite-service-model.md §2.2, CMP-006).

fulfillment.description: Who procures this constituent's request content, and when (UDLM ADR-009). platform = DCM's loop assembles it from consumer_fields at assembly-time (default); provider = the realizing provider brokers a sub-intent THROUGH DCM at realize-time, contributing realization-time specifics via a binding; consumer = the consumer supplies an existing resource reference (BYO). Orthogonal to provided_by (the realizer axis) — fulfillment is the procurement axis. DCM always orchestrates and applies policy + cycle detection regardless of mode.

depends_on.description: Sibling component_ids whose realized state must exist before this constituent can be dispatched — the ordering edges within the item (composite-service-model.md §2.3). The graph must be acyclic; forward topological order = dispatch, reverse = compensation/decommission. Sibling resolution and cycle rejection are validate.py-enforced.

bindings.description: DECLARED data movement (data-model-core §4): values pulled from a producer constituent's typed realized outputs into this constituent's spec at dispatch time. This is the typed, declared equivalent of the DCM control-plane's CEL ${component.output} wiring — data movement, not transformation (T4). A binding's from_component MUST also appear in this constituent's depends_on: data movement implies ordering (validate.py-enforced).

from_component.description: The producer constituent's component_id (a sibling within this item; validate.py-enforced).

output.description: The producer's declared output name — a key of its Resource Type Spec's typed outputs (E2).

to_field.description: Dot-path on THIS constituent's spec that receives the value at dispatch time (the catalog-level form of the instance schema's binds_to bound_field).

failure_effect.description: How this constituent's failure affects the composite outcome — the §2.4 required_for_delivery classification (composite-service-model.md): required = halt + compensate (composite FAILED); partial = record, continue (composite DEGRADED); optional = note, ignore. Evaluated by DCM, never by the registering provider (CMP-004).

spec_defaults.description: Free-form layer-assembled defaults for this constituent's spec — injected during the standard layer assembly phase (composite-service-model.md §7 step 3). Field values are typed by the constituent's Resource Type Spec, not re-validated here.

consumer_fields.description: The consumer-facing parameter surface: what a consumer supplies in the Intent record (composite-service-model.md §3.1) alongside the catalog_ref. Expansion maps these into constituent specs via layer assembly.

default.description: Default value when the consumer omits the field (any JSON type, matching `type`).

enum_values.description: Allowed values — required when type = enum.

metadata.description: Human-facing context, same shape as realized-entity: operational commentary lives in notes — data ABOUT the record, never used for resolution.

notes.description: Auditable operational notes: each entry is attributed and timestamped — never an anonymous mutable blob.

at.description: When recorded. RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

author.description: Who/what recorded it (person, agent+session, or process uuid).
